description: Create all required directory structures
steps: Create a new directory at src/hooks/ in the project root
Create a new directory at src/data/ in the project root
Create a new directory at src/components/ui/
Create a new directory at src/components/layout/
Create a new directory at src/components/sections/
Create a new directory at src/components/pages/
Verify all directories were created successfully by listing src/components/ contents

description: Create useToggle custom hook for managing boolean state
steps: Create a new file at src/hooks/useToggle.js
Write a custom hook function named useToggle that accepts an optional initialValue parameter defaulting to false
Inside the hook, use useState to create a value state variable initialized to initialValue
Create a toggle callback function using useCallback that inverts the current value when called
Return an array containing value, toggle, and setValue from the hook
Export the useToggle function as the default export
Add JSDoc comments documenting the hook parameters and return value

description: Create useScrollPosition custom hook for detecting scroll threshold
steps: Create a new file at src/hooks/useScrollPosition.js
Write a custom hook function named useScrollPosition that accepts an optional threshold parameter defaulting to 100
Inside the hook, use useState to create a scrolled state variable initialized to false
Use useEffect to add a scroll event listener that sets scrolled to true when window.scrollY exceeds threshold
Configure the scroll event listener with passive: true for performance
Return a cleanup function in useEffect that removes the scroll event listener
Return the scrolled boolean from the hook
Export the useScrollPosition function as the default export
Add JSDoc comments documenting the hook parameters and return value

description: Create useScrollReveal custom hook extracted from AboutReveal component
steps: Create a new file at src/hooks/useScrollReveal.js
Write a custom hook function named useScrollReveal that accepts a ref and an optional threshold parameter defaulting to 0.85
Inside the hook, use useState to create a isVisible state variable initialized to false
Use useEffect to check if the ref.current element exists before proceeding
Inside useEffect, create a handleScroll function that checks if the element's bounding rectangle top is less than window.innerHeight multiplied by threshold
When the visibility condition is met, set isVisible to true
Add the scroll event listener with passive: true
Call handleScroll once on mount to check initial visibility
Return a cleanup function that removes the scroll event listener
Return the isVisible boolean from the hook
Export the useScrollReveal function as the default export
Add JSDoc comments documenting the hook parameters and return value

description: Create useParallax custom hook for parallax scroll effects
steps: Create a new file at src/hooks/useParallax.js
Write a custom hook function named useParallax that accepts containerRef and imageRef as parameters
Inside the hook, use useEffect to check if both refs exist before proceeding
Check for prefers-reduced-motion media query and return early if user prefers reduced motion
Create a requestAnimationFrame-based scroll handler that calculates parallax translateY based on element position in viewport
Calculate progress as a value between 0 and 1 based on the container's scroll position
Apply a maxShift of 30 pixels to calculate the translateY offset
Set the --parallax-y CSS custom property on the image element with the calculated translateY value
Add scroll and resize event listeners with passive: true
Call the update function once on mount
Return a cleanup function that removes event listeners and cancels any pending requestAnimationFrame
Export the useParallax function as the default export
Add JSDoc comments documenting the hook parameters

description: Create useMediaQuery custom hook for responsive viewport detection
steps: Create a new file at src/hooks/useMediaQuery.js
Write a custom hook function named useMediaQuery that accepts a query string parameter
Inside the hook, use useState to create a matches state variable initialized to false
Use useEffect to create a MediaQueryList object using window.matchMedia with the provided query
Set the matches state to the initial matches property of the MediaQueryList
Add a change event listener to the MediaQueryList that updates matches state
Return a cleanup function that removes the change event listener
Return the matches boolean from the hook
Export the useMediaQuery function as the default export
Add JSDoc comments documenting the hook parameters and return value

description: Create hooks index file for centralized exports
steps: Create a new file at src/hooks/index.js
Import useToggle from ./useToggle
Import useScrollPosition from ./useScrollPosition
Import useScrollReveal from ./useScrollReveal
Import useParallax from ./useParallax
Import useMediaQuery from ./useMediaQuery
Export all hooks as named exports from the index file

description: PHASE 1 CHECK: Validate hooks build successfully
steps: Run npm run build to verify hooks do not cause build errors
Verify the build completes without errors related to the new hooks files
If build fails, fix any syntax or import errors before proceeding

description: Extract navigation links data from NavBar component
steps: Create a new file at src/data/navigation.js
Define a navLinks constant as an array of navigation link objects
Include a link object for Home with href '/' and label 'Home'
Include a link object for About with href '/about' and label 'About'
Include a link object for Services with href '/services', label 'Services', and a sections array

In the Services sections array, include an IP Solutions section with href '/service/ipsolutions', label 'IP Solutions', and items array containing 'Utility Patent Drawings', 'Design Patent Drawings', and 'Trademark Support'

In the Services sections array, include a Paralegal Solutions section with href '/service/paralegalsolutions', label 'Paralegal Solutions', and items array containing 'Case Management', 'Docketing Management', 'E-Filing Services', 'Contact Management Services', 'Trial Preparation', 'Deposition Coordination', and 'Compliance Support'
In the Services sections array, include a Custom Solutions section with href '/service/customsolutions' and label 'Custom Solutions'
Include a link object for FAQ with href '/#faq' and label 'FAQ'
Include a link object for Security with href '/security' and label 'Security'
Include a link object for Contact with href '/contact' and label 'Contact Us'
Export the navLinks constant as a named export

description: Extract IP services data from IpSolutionsPage component
steps: Create a new file at src/data/services.js
Define an ipServices constant as an array of service objects

Include a service object with image '/assets/stockimages/utilitypatentdrawing.jpg', title 'Utility Patent Drawings', and description 'USPTO, EPO, and WIPO-compliant utility illustrations crafted with technical precision and engineering expertise'

Include a service object with image '/assets/stockimages/desingpatentdrawing.jpg', title 'Design Patent Drawings', and description 'Accurate, visually compelling design patent illustrations that highlight unique visual qualities and distinct features'

Include a service object with image '/assets/stockimages/trademarkdesign.jpg', title 'Trademark Support', and description 'Comprehensive search, filing, and drawing services that capture the essence of your brand identity while meeting legal requirements'
Export the ipServices constant as a named export

description: Create data index file for centralized exports
steps: Create a new file at src/data/index.js
Import navLinks from ./navigation
Import ipServices from ./services
Export navLinks and ipServices as named exports from the index file

description: PHASE 2 CHECK: Validate data files build successfully
steps: Run npm run build to verify data files do not cause build errors
Verify the build completes without errors related to the new data files
If build fails, fix any syntax errors before proceeding

Verify the build completes without errors

description: Create NavContext for NavBar compound component pattern
steps: Create a new directory at src/components/layout/NavBar/
Create a new file at src/components/layout/NavBar/NavContext.js
Import createContext, useState, useCallback, useContext from react
Create a NavContext using createContext with undefined as the default value
Define a NavProvider component that accepts children as a prop
Inside NavProvider, create state for mobileMenuOpen using useState initialized to false
Create state for servicesDropdownOpen using useState initialized to false
Create state for activeDropdown using useState initialized to null
Create a toggleMobileMenu function using useCallback that toggles mobileMenuOpen
Create a closeMobileMenu function using useCallback that sets mobileMenuOpen and all sub-menus to false
Create a toggleServicesDropdown function using useCallback
Create a closeServicesDropdown function using useCallback
Create a context value object containing all state values and functions
Return NavContext.Provider wrapping the children with the context value
Create a custom hook useNavContext that uses useContext to access NavContext
Throw an error in useNavContext if used outside NavProvider with message 'useNavContext must be used within NavProvider'
Export NavProvider and useNavContext as named exports

description: Create ServicesDropdown component for NavBar
steps: Create a new file at src/components/layout/NavBar/ServicesDropdown.jsx
Add the 'use client' directive at the top of the file
Import Link from next/link
Import useNavContext from ./NavContext
Import the navLinks data from '../../../data/navigation'
Create a ServicesDropdown component function
Inside the component, destructure servicesDropdownOpen, closeServicesDropdown, and activeDropdown from useNavContext
Create a servicesTimeout ref using useRef for delay management
Add handleMouseEnter function that clears timeout and sets dropdown open
Add handleMouseLeave function that sets timeout to close dropdown after 120ms
Render a div with className 'services-dropdown' that only shows when servicesDropdownOpen is true
Inside the dropdown, find the Services item from navLinks and map over its sections
For each section, render a div with className 'services-dropdown-section'
Render the section title as a Link with className 'services-dropdown-title'
Render a ul list of items for each section
Add onMouseEnter and onMouseLeave handlers to both trigger and dropdown for hover behavior
Export the ServicesDropdown component as default

description: Create MobileMenu component for NavBar
steps: Create a new file at src/components/layout/NavBar/MobileMenu.jsx
Add the 'use client' directive at the top of the file
Import Link from next/link
Import useNavContext from ./NavContext
Import useToggle from '../../../hooks/useToggle'
Import the navLinks data from '../../../data/navigation'
Create a MobileMenu component function
Inside the component, destructure mobileMenuOpen, closeMobileMenu from useNavContext
Create local toggle state for IP solutions dropdown using useToggle
Create local toggle state for Paralegal solutions dropdown using useToggle
Create local toggle state for Custom solutions dropdown using useToggle
Render a div with className 'mobile-nav' that conditionally applies 'active' class based on mobileMenuOpen
Inside the mobile nav, render a div with className 'mobile-nav-content'
Render a ul with className 'mobile-nav-links'
Map over navLinks and render each as a list item with Link
For Services link, render a special expandable section with toggle button and chevron icon
Inside Services, render expandable sections for IP Solutions, Paralegal Solutions, and Custom Solutions
Each sub-section should have a toggle button that calls the corresponding toggle function
Ensure all Link components call closeMobileMenu when clicked
Render a div with className 'mobile-nav-contact' containing a Button component
Export the MobileMenu component as default

description: Refactor NavBar.jsx to use compound component pattern
steps: Create a new file at src/components/layout/NavBar/NavBar.jsx
Add the 'use client' directive at the top of the file
Import Link from next/link
Import usePathname from next/navigation
Import NavProvider from ./NavContext
Import useNavContext from ./NavContext
Import ServicesDropdown from ./ServicesDropdown
Import MobileMenu from './MobileMenu'
Import Button from '../../ui/Button'
Import useScrollPosition from '../../../hooks/useScrollPosition'
Import useMediaQuery from '../../../hooks/useMediaQuery'
Read the current src/styles/NavBar.css and copy its contents
Create src/components/layout/NavBar/NavBar.css with the copied contents
Import the CSS from './NavBar.css'
Create a DesktopNav internal component that renders desktop navigation links
Inside DesktopNav, use useNavContext to get servicesDropdownOpen and dropdown handlers
Render nav links from navLinks data using map
For Services link, wrap in a li with mouse enter/leave handlers
Render ServicesDropdown inside the Services li
Create a HamburgerButton internal component for mobile menu toggle
Inside HamburgerButton, use useNavContext to get toggleMobileMenu and mobileMenuOpen
Render a div with className 'mobile-menu-btn' and onClick handler
Inside, render a div with className 'hamburger' that toggles 'active' class
Render three span elements inside hamburger for the three lines
Create the main NavBar component function
Inside NavBar, use useScrollPosition hook to get scrolled state
Use useMediaQuery hook with '(max-width: 768px)' to detect mobile viewport
Get the current pathname using usePathname
Calculate isHomePage as pathname === '/'
Calculate shouldShowTransparent as isHomePage && !scrolled
Return NavProvider wrapping the entire navigation
Inside NavProvider, render nav element with dynamic className based on shouldShowTransparent
Inside nav, render Link to '/' with className 'navbar-logo' containing logo image and text
Render DesktopNav component
Render a div with className 'navbar-contact' containing Button
Render HamburgerButton component
Render MobileMenu component outside the nav element but inside NavProvider
Export the NavBar component as default

description: Create NavBar index file for exports
steps: Create a new file at src/components/layout/NavBar/index.js
Add an export statement: export { default } from './NavBar'
Add an export statement: export { default as NavBar } from './NavBar'

Remove the 'use client' directive if the component can be a server component, otherwise keep it
Check if src/styles/ContactUs.css exists and copy to src/components/pages/ContactPage/ContactPage.css
Update the CSS import to './ContactPage.css'
Update all component imports to use correct relative paths
Create a new file at src/components/pages/ContactPage/index.js
Add an export statement that re-exports the default export from ContactPage.jsx
Do NOT delete original files yet

description: Create pages index file for centralized exports
steps: Create a new file at src/components/pages/index.js
Import AboutPage from './AboutPage'
Import IpSolutionsPage from './IpSolutionsPage'
Import ParalegalSolutionsPage from './ParalegalSolutionsPage'
Import CustomSolutionsPage from './CustomSolutionsPage'
Import ServicesPage from './ServicesPage'
Import SecurityPage from './SecurityPage'
Import ContactPage from './ContactPage'
Export all page components as named exports from the index file

description: PHASE 6 CHECK: Validate pages components build successfully
steps: Run npm run build to verify pages components do not cause build errors
Verify the build completes without errors
If build fails, fix any import path or server/client component errors before proceeding

description: Update src/app/layout.js to import NavBar from new location

Find the NavBar import statement
Update the NavBar import to: import NavBar from '../components/layout/NavBar'
Verify no other imports need updating in layout.js

Update OurSection import to: import OurSection from '../components/sections/OurSection'

Update FotterSection import to: import Footer from '../components/layout/Footer'
Update HeroActions import to: import HeroActions from '../components/ui/HeroActions' (create index if needed)
Add HeroSection import: import HeroSection from '../components/sections/HeroSection'
Replace the hero section JSX in the return statement with <HeroSection />
Update any JSX that uses <FotterSection /> to use <Footer /> instead
Remove the renderAnimatedWords function definition if it was moved to HeroSection

description: PHASE 8 CHECK: Validate cleanup did not break the build
steps: Run npm run build to verify deleting old files did not cause build errors
Verify the build completes without 'Module not found' errors
If build fails, check if any files were still being imported from old locations and fix
Run npm run dev and verify the development server starts without errors

description: Add keyboard navigation accessibility to ServicesDropdown
steps: Read src/components/layout/NavBar/ServicesDropdown.jsx
Add state for focusedItemIndex using useState initialized to -1
Add a focusableItems ref array to store all focusable elements in the dropdown
Create a handleKeyDown function
In handleKeyDown, for ArrowDown: preventDefault, increment focusedItemIndex, and focus the next item
In handleKeyDown, for ArrowUp: preventDefault, decrement focusedItemIndex, and focus the previous item
In handleKeyDown, for Enter: activate the currently focused link
In handleKeyDown, for Escape: call closeServicesDropdown and return focus to the Services link
Add onKeyDown={handleKeyDown} to the dropdown container div
Add role='menu' to the dropdown container div
Add role='menuitem' to each Link inside the dropdown
Add tabIndex={-1} to each Link so they can be focused programmatically
Add aria-expanded={servicesDropdownOpen} to the Services trigger link in NavBar.jsx
Add aria-haspopup='true' to the Services trigger link in NavBar.jsx

description: Add accessibility attributes to MobileMenu component
steps: Read src/components/layout/NavBar/MobileMenu.jsx
Add role='navigation' to the outer mobile-nav div
Add aria-label='Main navigation' to the mobile-nav div
Read NavBar.jsx and add aria-expanded attribute to the hamburger button that reflects mobileMenuOpen state
Add aria-controls='mobile-navigation' to the hamburger button
Add id='mobile-navigation' to the mobile-nav div in MobileMenu.jsx
Add aria-label='Toggle navigation menu' to the hamburger button
Add aria-hidden={!mobileMenuOpen} to the mobile-nav div to hide from screen readers when closed
For each expandable section toggle button, add aria-expanded attribute reflecting its open state
Add aria-controls attribute to each toggle button referencing the id of the section it controls
Ensure all toggle buttons have accessible labels via aria-label

description: Add accessibility attributes to Button component
steps: Read src/components/ui/Button/Button.jsx
Add an ariaLabel optional prop to the component
When rendering button element, add aria-label={ariaLabel} if ariaLabel is provided
When rendering Link element, add aria-label={ariaLabel} if ariaLabel is provided
Ensure type='button' is the default when no type prop is specified
Add a disabled optional prop if not already present
When button is disabled, add aria-disabled='true' attribute
Add JSDoc documentation for the new ariaLabel and disabled props

description: PHASE 9 CHECK: Validate accessibility changes build successfully
steps: Run npm run build to verify accessibility changes do not cause build errors
Verify the build completes without errors
If build fails, fix any syntax errors before proceeding

description: Run development server and verify all pages load correctly
steps: Run npm run dev to start the development server
Open browser and navigate to http://localhost:3000/
Verify the home page loads without errors in the browser console
Navigate to /about and verify it loads correctly
Navigate to /services and verify it loads correctly
Navigate to /service/ipsolutions and verify it loads correctly
Navigate to /service/paralegalsolutions and verify it loads correctly
Navigate to /service/customsolutions and verify it loads correctly
Navigate to /security and verify it loads correctly
Navigate to /contact and verify it loads correctly
Open browser developer tools and check for any console errors
Check for any hydration errors in the console

description: Test navigation functionality
steps: With dev server running, test the desktop navigation by hovering over Services
Verify the Services dropdown appears on hover
Click each link in the dropdown and verify navigation works
Test the mobile navigation by resizing browser to mobile width or using dev tools
Click the hamburger menu button and verify mobile menu opens
Test expanding and collapsing the Services section in mobile menu
Test expanding and collapsing each sub-section (IP Solutions, Paralegal, Custom)
Click links in mobile menu and verify they navigate correctly
Verify mobile menu closes after clicking a link
Test the 'Get In Touch' button in navbar and verify it works

description: Test keyboard navigation and accessibility
steps: With dev server running, press Tab key to navigate through the home page
Verify the focus order is logical (logo, nav links, buttons, content)
Verify all interactive elements can be focused
Press Enter on focused links and buttons to activate them
Navigate to Services link and press Tab to enter dropdown if open
Test ArrowDown and ArrowUp keys in the Services dropdown
Press Escape to close the dropdown
Test opening mobile menu with keyboard (Tab to hamburger, Enter)
Test closing mobile menu with Escape key
Verify no keyboard traps exist
Test at different viewport sizes (resize browser)

description: Test CSS styles and visual appearance
steps: With dev server running, visually inspect each page
Verify that all colors match the design (check CSS variables are applied)
Verify typography and fonts are correct
Verify spacing and layout match the original
Test responsive design by resizing browser to different widths
Verify mobile layout looks correct
Verify tablet layout looks correct
Verify desktop layout looks correct
Check that animations still work (AboutReveal scroll reveal)
Check that ServicesHeroParallax effects work
Check that hover states on buttons and links work
Check browser console for any CSS loading errors

description: Run production build and verify
steps: Stop the development server
Run npm run build to create a production build
Verify the build completes successfully with no errors
Check the build output for any warnings
Run npm start to start the production server
Navigate to http://localhost:3000/ in browser
Verify the home page loads correctly in production
Navigate through all pages and verify they work
Check browser console for any errors in production
Verify no hydration errors appear
Verify the production build is optimized (check for lazy loading, etc.)

description: Final folder structure verification and documentation
steps: List the contents of src/components/ and verify it contains only: ui/, layout/, sections/, pages/
List the contents of src/components/ui/ and verify all UI components are present
List the contents of src/components/layout/ and verify NavBar/ and Footer/ are present
List the contents of src/components/sections/ and verify all sections are present
List the contents of src/components/pages/ and verify all page components are present
List the contents of src/hooks/ and verify all hooks are present
List the contents of src/data/ and verify all data files are present
List the contents of src/styles/ and verify only globals.css and variables.css remain
Remove any empty directories
Document the final folder structure in a summary
